threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot the newcomers—they're the ones still looking puzzled at the bus stops despite the 'Next Bus' screen showing delays.
Last Saturday, the 35 bus was packed with a group singing old Skints songs—plenty of shushing from the driver but no one cared.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found an old lady selling homemade jam on the corner of my street, while a group of kids played Fußball nearby. That’s Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a vegan döner after a night out, while the street art around you keeps changing every week.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can name at least three taco trucks and remember the one that went missing in '17.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy reading a newspaper on the bus this morning, completely unfazed by the chaos of street performers and tourists outside. Classic San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about bars and restaurants, but many locals still have a thriving community life centered around small plazas and neighborhood traditions.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they always stand on the wrong side of the escalator, blocking the locals in a hurry.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still ask for directions to the nearest tube station like it’s a normal thing. Locals just point you to the nearest Overground.
Caught a night bus from Brixton and saw a couple arguing over a lost takeaway. He dropped the chips and she was more upset about the sauce.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was home? Watching a group of teenagers turn an empty lot into a pop-up skate park, complete with a boombox and graffiti art.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only here will you find a bakery that opens at midnight for fresh bread and pastries, serving night owls and late-shift workers. It's a game changer.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local won't flinch when a taco truck runs out of al pastor; they just swap for the asada without missing a beat.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy bringing a live chicken on the J Line. Only in San Francisco could that be a normal Tuesday commute.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals spend weekends at Park Güell; in reality, we avoid it like the plague and seek out hidden plazas instead.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still taking photos of everything, especially the street art. You can tell by their slow pace and wide eyes.
